task_description: Implement unique_paths(m, n): in an m × n grid, count the number of unique paths from top-left (0,0) to bottom-right (m-1, n-1) moving only right or down. For m <= 0 or n <= 0, return 0. For m == 1 and n == 1, return 1.

bugs_in_buggy.description: Right-hand `dp[i][j]` should be `dp[i][j-1]`; the recurrence as written reads the cell being assigned, not the cell to the left.
No early return for m <= 0 or n <= 0; building `[[1]*0 for _ in range(0)]` then indexing dp[-1][-1] yields wrong/None result depending on dimensions.
bugs_in_buggy.suggested_fix: Guard with `if m <= 0 or n <= 0: return 0`.

bugs_in_tricky.description: Using a padded (m+1, n+1) table with `dp[0][*]=dp[*][0]=1` then returning dp[m][n] over-counts: dp[1][1] becomes 2 (incorrect for the 1x1 grid). The padding scheme is internally inconsistent.
bugs_in_tricky.suggested_fix: Either drop the padding (use m × n, initialize first row/col to 1, return dp[m-1][n-1]) or use dp[0][0]=1 and dp[*][*]=0 elsewhere, but not both.
